title: Sandbox Isolation
subtitle: Sandbox Isolation
description: How to isolate the execution environment when Agent runs code to prevent host machine pollution. Balancing security and convenience.
sub_problems: Code execution isolation: User code runs in sandbox without affecting the host machine
File system isolation: Agent can only access designated directories
Network isolation: Restrict Agent's network access scope
Resource limits: Constraints on CPU, memory, and execution time
Environment consistency: Consistency between sandbox environment and target environment
Import control: Restrict importable modules to prevent dangerous API calls
Intent-to-code isolation: LLM converts user intent to code before execution, avoiding direct injection
best_practices: Dual-layer abstraction is best practice: Provider manages lifecycle, Sandbox manages operations; DeerFlow's pattern is worth learning
Virtual paths must translate bidirectionally: input translation + output reverse translation, otherwise Agent sees real paths and gets confused
Lazy initialization saves resources: not all Agents need sandbox, create only on first tool call
Set execution timeout: MiroThinker 600s, blog scenario 30s, prevent infinite loops from exhausting resources
Defend against LLM hallucination: INVALID_SANDBOX_IDS blacklist is simple and effective defense
Result truncation protection: auto-truncate output exceeding threshold to avoid token explosion
AST static analysis import whitelist is zero-overhead pre-execution security check, suitable to supplement process isolation
Configuration loading priority chain (environment variables > module config > global config > defaults) allows same codebase to adapt to multiple deployment environments
LLM intent→code conversion is safer than direct user input execution; ToolAgent's _generate_code_from_intent pattern is worth learning

values.DeerFlow: Provider+Sandbox dual-layer abstraction (Local subprocess / Aio Docker)
values.DeepTutor: Process-level: subprocess.run + tempfile temporary directory, production environment stacked with Docker container
No virtual path translation, direct allowed_roots whitelist validation on real paths

values.DeerFlow: SandboxMiddleware lazy initialization + idle timeout recovery 600s
values.DeepTutor: Lazy initialization + TemporaryDirectory context manager auto-cleanup
Three-layer defense in depth: ImportGuard(AST) + path whitelist + subprocess timeout
ToolAgent detects exit_code failure then adds error prefix hint for path correction

solutions.title: SandboxProvider ABC + Virtual Path Mapping + Middleware Lifecycle
solutions.description: Classic Provider + Sandbox dual-layer abstraction: SandboxProvider(ABC) manages sandbox lifecycle (acquire/get/release), Sandbox(ABC) provides 5 tools (bash/ls/read_file/write_file/str_replace). LocalSandbox uses subprocess + local FS (global singleton), AioSandbox uses HTTP API → Docker/Apple Container. Virtual path system translates /mnt/user-data/workspace/* bidirectionally (tool layer replace_virtual_path + sandbox layer _resolve_path + output reverse translation _reverse_resolve_paths_in_output). SandboxMiddleware controls lazy initialization (lazy_init=True defers to first tool call), AioSandbox supports deterministic ID sha256(thread_id)[:8], idle timeout recovery 600s, atexit graceful shutdown.

solutions.title: E2B Code Interpreter Cloud Sandbox + LLM Auto-Repair
solutions.description: python_mcp_server.py provides cloud code execution environment via e2b_code_interpreter.Sandbox. Core constants: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=600s single execution timeout, MAX_RESULT_LEN=20000 character result truncation, MAX_ERROR_LEN=4000 character error truncation. INVALID_SANDBOX_IDS blacklist (default/sandbox1/sandbox/some_id/new_sandbox) prevents LLM from fabricating sandbox IDs. Supports file upload to sandbox. Each task gets independent Sandbox instance without interference. Migration adaptation as CodeValidatorService: timeout reduced to 30s, result truncation reduced to 5000 characters, validate_and_fix() uses LLM auto-repair on validation failure (max 2 attempts).

solutions.title: Path Whitelist Approach
solutions.description: Agent system needs to read and write files when executing research tasks: saving search results, generating reports, caching intermediate data. Unrestricted file access introduces security risks.

solutions.title: Process-Level Workspace Isolation Approach
solutions.description: Lightweight process isolation in educational scenarios: no external sandbox service dependency, implements application-layer security through subprocess + AST inspection
